LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. RULING: A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. PROVING ACTUAL DAMAGES IS AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF A MALPRACTICE CLAIM. "THE MERE BREACH OF A PROFESSIONAL DUTY, CAUSING ONLY NOMINAL DAMAGES, SPECULATIVE HARM, OR THE THREAT OF FUTURE HARM - HARM NOT YET REALIZED - DOES NOT SUFFICE TO CREATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE." (BUDD V. NIXEN (1971) 6 CAL.3D 195, 200.) DEFENDANT MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN BY PRESENTING EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NO EVIDENCE OF ACTUAL DAMAGES. (DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS 18-22.) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT IN OPPOSITION. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS F AND G TO THE DECLARATION OF ORVILLE MEAUX ARE SUSTAINED. EXHIBIT F, A PURPORTED COPY OF A FORENSIC ACCOUNTING PERFORMED AT THE REQUEST OF PLAINTIFF BY HIS EXPERT, IS INADMISSIBLE BECAUSE THERE IS NO DECLARATION FROM THE EXPERT EXPLAINING THE EXPERT'S QUALIFICATIONS, WHAT HE RELIED ON IN PUTTING TOGETHER THE SPREADSHEET, OR HOW THE SPREADSHEET APPLIES TO THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. (SEE KELLY V. TRUNK (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 519, 523-24.) EXHIBIT G, A DECLARATION FROM PLAINTIFF'S HUMAN RESOURCES EXPERT, IS INADMISSIBLE BECAUSE IT IS NOT EXECUTED UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY. (CCP SEC. 2015.5; KULSHRESTHA V. FIRST UNION COMMERCIAL CORPORATION (2014) 33 CAL.4TH 601.) THUS, THE ONLY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED ON THE ISSUE OF ACTUAL DAMAGES IS PLAINTIFF'S OWN OPINION OF WHAT HE BELIEVES THE UNDERLYING MALPRACTICE CASE WAS WORTH. THIS IS INSUFFICIENT TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. (SEE MARSHAK V. BALLESTEROS (1999) 72 CAL.APP.4TH 1514, 1519.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302.

Defendant Paul J. Steiners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jason Fellner of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney telephone number 415-788-1900 for Defendant (moving party).
Kendra Basner of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP telephone number 415-362-6000 for Third Party Richard Duane.

The Parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, Defendant Paul J. Steiner's Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records is denied as moot. Summary judgment was granted to the Defendant. Request for sanctions is denied. The moving Party is to prepare the order.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bert Carter, Jr., Attorney at Law, Tel. 510.465.7727, for plaintiffs;
Geoffrey T. MacBride, of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788-1900, for defendants;
Jason E. Fellner, of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788-1900, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Proving actual damages is an essential element of a malpractice claim. "The mere breach of a professional duty, causing only nominal damages, speculative harm, or the threat of future harm - harm not yet realized - does not suffice to create a cause of action for negligence." (Budd v. Nixen (1971) 6 Cal.3d 195, 200.) Defendant met his initial burden by presenting evidence showing that Plaintiff has no evidence of actual damages. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 18-22.) Plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of material fact in opposition. Defendant's objections to Exhibits F and G to the Declaration of Orville Meaux are sustained. Exhibit F, a purported copy of a forensic accounting performed at the request of Plaintiff by his expert, is inadmissible because there is no declaration from the expert explaining the expert's qualifications, what he relied on in putting together the spreadsheet, or how the spreadsheet applies to the facts of this case. (See Kelly v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 523-24.) Exhibit G, a declaration from Plaintiff's human resources expert, is inadmissible because it is not executed under penalty of perjury. (CCP sec. 2015.5; Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corporation (2014) 33 Cal.4th 601.) Thus, the only admissible evidence submitted on the issue of actual damages is Plaintiff's own opinion of what he believes the underlying malpractice case was worth. This is insufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact. (See Marshak v. Ballesteros (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 1514, 1519.). Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
